LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, LI HUANG'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT REGARDING WHETHER THE OFF DUTY MUNI OPERATORS WERE ACTING IN THE COURSE AND SCOPE OF THEIR EMPLOYMENT AND WHETHER THEY BREACHED THEIR DUTY OF CARE. (ISSUE 1) THE OFF-DUTY OFFICERS WERE UNIFORMED, WENT TO THE BUS YARD PRIOR TO BOARDING THE BUS, AND RODE FOR FREE. THERE IS A FACTUAL ISSUE WHETHER THEIR CONDUCT WAS IN FURTHERANCE OF DEFENDANT'S ENTERPRISE. SEE DEMIRJIAN V. IDEAL HEATING CORP. (1954) 129 CAL. APP. 2D 758, 765. SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ON THE NEGLIGENT TRAINING CAUSE OF ACTION (ISSUE 4) IS DENIED. IT CANNOT BE SAID AS A MATTER OF LAW THAT THERE IS NO SUCH CAUSE OF ACTION UNDER THE FACTS OF THIS CASE. SEE C.A. V. WILLIAM S. HART UNION HIGH SCHOOL DISTRICT (2012) 53 CAL. 4TH 861. AS TO ISSUE 5, THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT AS TO DEFENDANT HUANG'S NEGLIGENCE. THE INCIDENT TOOK APPROXIMATELY 20 MINUTES (PLAINTIFF'S DECL. PARA 16); HUANG ADMITS HIS MIRROR WAS POSITIONED TO FOCUS ON THE FRONT OF THE BUS (HUANG DEPO., PP. 25-26), AND THERE IS A FACTUAL ISSUE WHETHER HE SHOULD HAVE KNOWN THAT AN ASSAULT WAS ABOUT TO OCCUR. AS TO ISSUE 6, THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE AS TO WHETHER OFF DUTY PERSONNEL BREACHED A DUTY. THE OFF DUTY PERSONNEL WERE SITTING NEAR PLAINTIFF AND OBSERVED THE WHOLE EXCHANGE. (PLAINTIFF'S DECL. PARAS. 10, 11, 13, 19.) ISSUES 2 AND 3 ARE MOOT, IN LIGHT OF PLAINTIFF'S DISMISSAL PREVIOUSLY FILED. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER #5976.

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Oct-03-2013 Vacated

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Apr-09-2013 Continued To Oct-07-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Apr-09-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: SHAANA A. RAHMAN, ESQ., 415.956.9245, FOR PLAINTIFF CHRISTOPHER BORGZINNER.
JAMES F. HANNAWALT AND DAVID A. DELBON, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEYS, 415.554.3800, FOR DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling WITH MODIFICATION: DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, LI HUANG'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. There is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether the off duty Muni operators were acting in the course and scope of their employment and whether they breached their duty of care. (Issue 1) The off-duty officers were uniformed, went to the bus yard prior to boarding the bus, and rode for free. There is a factual issue whether their conduct was in furtherance of Defendant's enterprise. See DeMirjian v. Ideal Heating Corp. (1954) 129 Cal. App. 2d 758, 765. Summary adjudication on the negligent training cause of action (issue 4) is denied. It cannot be said as a matter of law that there is no such cause of action under the facts of this case. See C.A. v. William S. Hart Union High School District (2012) 53 Cal. 4th 861. As to issue 5, there is a triable issue of material fact as to defendant Huang's negligence. The incident took approximately 20 minutes (Plaintiff's decl. para 16); Huang admits his mirror was positioned to focus on the front of the bus (Huang Depo., pp. 25-26), and there is a factual issue whether he should have known that an assault was about to occur. As to issue 6, there is a triable issue as to whether off duty personnel breached a duty. The off duty personnel were sitting near Plaintiff and observed the whole exchange. (Plaintiff's decl. paras. 10, 11, 13, 19.) Issues 2 and 3 are moot, in light of plaintiff's dismissal previously filed. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer #5976.
Defendant City And County Of San Francisco, Li Huang Motion For Summary Judgment

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
